alkylation reagent: the resulting lysates reacted with 1 mM CuSO4, 100 µM TBTA ligand, 100 µM azide-biotin, and 1 mM TCEP for 1 h at room temperature.
alkylation concentration: the resulting lysates reacted with 1 mM CuSO4, 100 µM TBTA ligand, 100 µM azide-biotin, and 1 mM TCEP for 1 h at room temperature.
cleavage agent: the conidiogenone C-IRGM1 complex was digested with trypsin and the conidiogenone C-labeled IRGM1 fragment was further detected by LC-MS/MS analysis.
enrichment method: the resulting lysates reacted with 1 mM CuSO4, 100 µM TBTA ligand, 100 µM azide-biotin, and 1 mM TCEP for 1 h at room temperature.
instrument: the LC-MS/MS analysis of digestion samples was performed on LC-MS/MS using Orbitrap Fusion™ Lumos™ (Thermo scientific, USA).
labeling: the labeled proteins were identified and quantified by SDS-PAGE or LC-MS/MS analysis.
reduction reagent: the resulting lysates reacted with 1 mM CuSO4, 100 µM TBTA ligand, 100 µM azide-biotin, and 1 mM TCEP for 1 h at room temperature.
reduction concentration: the resulting lysates reacted with 1 mM CuSO4, 100 µM TBTA ligand, 100 µM azide-biotin, and 1 mM TCEP for 1 h at room temperature.
